identifiers: # Example transfer configurations
# Uncomment and modify the lines below, or add your own
# Column descriptions:
#   identifiers      - Unique transfer ID used to name the generated .sh script
#   enabled          - TRUE to enable this transfer, FALSE to disable
#   system           - Hostname or identifier for the system running this transfer
#   notes            - Optional notes/comments about this transfer
#   users            - Username running the cron job
#   source           - Source path (supports wildcards like input/*)
#   source_port      - SSH port for remote sources (leave empty for default 22)
#   destination      - Destination path (local or remote user@host:/path/)
#   destination_port - SSH port for remote destinations (leave empty for default 22)
#   rsync_options    - Additional rsync options (e.g., --chown=:group --chmod=Du=rwx)
#   io_nice          - Optional ionice args/command for rsync (e.g. -c2 -n7; empty skips ionice)
#   log_file         - Log file name under the system log folder (e.g. transfers.log)
#   flock_file       - Lock file name under the system flock folder (required)
#   frequency         - Cron schedule (default: */15 * * * * = every 15 minutes)
#   flow_group        - Optional flow identifier for portable run tracking and validation wrappers
#   is_entry_point    - TRUE when this transfer is the first hop that creates flow metadata
#   is_end_point      - TRUE when this transfer is a terminal hop in a tracked flow
#   notify_on_success - TRUE to send a notification when this transfer completes, if notifications.endpoint is configured
#   notify_on_error   - TRUE to send a notification when this transfer errors, if notifications.endpoint is configured
#   test_fixture_names - Optional comma-separated fixture directories used by integration validation
#   tags              - Optional comma-separated labels for reporting and separation validation
# Frequency examples:
#   */5 * * * *   - every 5 minutes
#   */15 * * * *  - every 15 minutes
#   0 * * * *     - every hour
#   0 0 * * *     - daily at midnight
#   0 */6 * * *   - every 6 hours
# Local transfer example (uncomment to use):
transfer_001
# Remote transfer example (enable and modify):
transfer_002
